column1: Matthew: Jesus appears to women and disciples in Galilee.
Luke: Focuses on appearances in and around Jerusalem (Emmaus, Upper Room).
John: Includes unique appearances (Mary Magdalene at the tomb, Thomas’ doubt, breakfast by the Sea of Galilee).
Acts: Summarizes appearances and describes the Ascension from the Mount of Olives.

The table above captures the full sweep of Jesus’ life, ministry, betrayal, death, and resurrection as recorded in the canonical Gospels and Acts, including all major variations and the fate of Judas Iscariot.
Judas’ actions and death are essential in the Passion narrative, fulfilling prophecy and serving as a warning about betrayal and remorse.
Variations across the Gospels reflect different theological emphases and traditions, but all agree on the core events: Jesus’ ministry, crucifixion, and resurrection.
For further detail, see harmonized Gospel timelines and scholarly commentaries for in-depth analysis of each event’s scriptural variations and significance.
